cases.notes: User goes through all 4 phases providing rich input. Exercises goal enrichment, project FK linkage, week outcome write, and preferences merge. Agent call is Phase 1 only; Phases 2–4 are form writes. Expected: all 4 entity tables populated, first_run_complete=true.

goal_titles: Ship a product people rely on
input.week_outcome: Have the onboarding flow working end-to-end and demoed
input.journal_text: Starting Intently because I keep losing track of what actually matters week to week.

must_surface: Agent returns slices array with exactly 3 items indexed 0, 1, 2.
Each slice has a non-empty monthly_slice (under 40 words) grounded in the user's goal title language.
Each slice has a glyph from the canonical set (leaf, star, flame, bolt, heart, shield, anchor, compass, mountain, sun).
goals table receives 3 rows with title, monthly_slice, glyph, palette, position.
projects table receives 2 rows with goal_id FK matching the inserted goal IDs, plus the hardcoded Admin project.
life_ops_config.config contains this_week_outcome matching the input string.
life_ops_config.config contains work_hours.start='09:00', work_hours.end='18:00', energy_pattern='morning_peak', daily_brief_time='07:00', weekly_review_day='Sunday'.
life_ops_config.config.first_run_complete=true.
entries table receives 1 row with kind='journal' and body_markdown matching the journal_text.
anti_patterns: Agent invents goals the user did not provide.
monthly_slice paraphrases or reframes the goal into different language (e.g. 'Establish a sustainable product culture').
glyph outside the canonical set.
projects written without goal_id when a goal_index was provided.
first_run_complete left false or absent after final persist.
Journal entry written even when journal_text is blank.

cases.notes: User skips Phase 2 entirely (no projects entered). Only the hardcoded Admin project should be written. Goals, outcome, and prefs still written.

must_surface: Agent returns 2 slices indexed 0 and 1.
goals table receives 2 rows.
projects table receives only the Admin row (is_admin=true) — no user projects.
life_ops_config.config.this_week_outcome = 'Draft three essay outlines'.
life_ops_config.config.first_run_complete = true.
No entries row written (journal_text was blank).
anti_patterns: Any non-Admin project row inserted when the user provided no projects.
Error thrown because projects array is empty.
journal entry inserted when journal_text is empty string.

cases.notes: User accepts all Phase 4 defaults without changing anything. Tests that defaults are persisted correctly even when the user doesn't modify the form.

must_surface: Agent returns 1 slice for the single goal.
goals table receives 1 row.
projects table receives the user project + Admin.
life_ops_config.config.work_hours = { start: '09:00', end: '18:00' }.
life_ops_config.config.energy_pattern = 'unset'.
life_ops_config.config.daily_brief_time = '07:00'.
life_ops_config.config.weekly_review_day = 'Sunday'.
life_ops_config.config does NOT contain this_week_outcome key (or it is absent/null) because week_outcome was blank.
first_run_complete = true.
anti_patterns: this_week_outcome written as empty string rather than omitted.
Defaults overwritten with null/undefined values.
Error on single-goal input.

cases.notes: User completes Phase 1 (goals entered, agent called, goals_review shown), then hard-refreshes. On remount, SetupFlow should detect setup_draft with phase=1 in life_ops_config.config and show the resume prompt. Clicking 'Continue' loads enriched goals into state and advances to the projects step (Phase 2). This verifies draft persistence after Phase 1.

must_surface: After Phase 1 agent call completes, life_ops_config.config.setup_draft.phase = 1.
setup_draft.goals contains the entered goal titles.
setup_draft.enriched contains the agent-enriched goal objects.
On remount (simulated refresh), SetupFlow shows the resume_prompt step — not intro.
Resume prompt displays 'Continue from Phase 2'.
Clicking 'Continue' loads enriched goals into state and sets step to 'projects'.
No zombie resume prompt appears after Phase 4 completion (setup_draft is cleared).
anti_patterns: Draft is written BEFORE the agent call completes (draft should only be saved after agent succeeds).
Resume prompt appears for a user with first_run_complete=true and no active draft.
Clicking 'Start over' leaves setup_draft intact in the DB.
Crash or blank screen when draft.enriched is present and loaded into state.

cases.notes: User completes Phase 1, refreshes, sees resume prompt, but clicks 'Start over'. Verifies the draft is cleared from the DB and the user starts cleanly at Phase 1 (goals_input step), with no prior data pre-filled.

goal_titles: Old goal that should be discarded

must_surface: After 'Start over' click, life_ops_config.config.setup_draft is null/absent.
SetupFlow renders the goals_input step with blank goal fields.
No previously entered goals are pre-filled in the inputs.
User can enter new goals and complete setup normally.
anti_patterns: Draft still present in DB after 'Start over'.
Old goal text visible in goal inputs after starting over.
Another resume prompt appears on a subsequent refresh after 'Start over'.

cases.notes: User completes all 4 phases. After Phase 4 save (finalPersist), setup_draft must be cleared. A subsequent page refresh must NOT show a resume prompt.

must_surface: After finalPersist completes, life_ops_config.config.setup_draft is null/absent.
life_ops_config.config.first_run_complete = true.
On a subsequent SetupFlow mount (simulated refresh), step initializes to 'intro' — no resume_prompt.
goals, projects, plan_items rows exist for the user.
anti_patterns: setup_draft remains non-null after successful Phase 4 completion.
Resume prompt appears after full setup completion on refresh.
clearSetupDraft failure causes the UI to show an error (it should be best-effort/non-blocking).

cases.notes: User provides fully non-default preferences: early brief time, non-Sunday review day, explicit energy pattern, and narrow work hours.

input.week_outcome: Complete the first two chapters of the systems design book
input.journal_text: I've tried tracking systems before and they all fall apart. Hoping this one actually sticks because it's agent-powered.

must_surface: Agent returns 2 slices indexed 0 and 1.
goals table receives 2 rows.
projects table receives 3 user projects + Admin. The third project (goal_index=null) is inserted with null goal_id.
life_ops_config.config.focus_area = 'Engineering growth'.
life_ops_config.config.work_hours = { start: '07:30', end: '16:30' }.
life_ops_config.config.daily_brief_time = '06:00'.
life_ops_config.config.energy_pattern = 'morning_peak'.
life_ops_config.config.weekly_review_day = 'Friday'.
life_ops_config.config.this_week_outcome = 'Complete the first two chapters of the systems design book'.
entries row inserted with kind='journal' matching the journal_text.
first_run_complete = true.
anti_patterns: Non-default prefs silently reverted to defaults.
Third project errors out because goal_index is null — it should insert with null goal_id.
monthly_slice for 'Run a half-marathon' reframed into general wellness language rather than running-specific.
Journal entry not written despite non-empty journal_text.
